preview_text:
The model identifies Portugal as a slight favorite at 35% to win, but the margin over Colombia is narrow—less than 2 percentage points. Colombia's 34% win probability positions this as a genuinely competitive fixture rather than a clear hierarchy between the two sides.

The win-draw-loss spread reflects a tightly balanced encounter. Draws account for 32% of the model's distribution, a substantial share that underscores how evenly matched these teams appear in the statistical forecast. The separation between any single outcome remains modest, suggesting the match could plausibly resolve in any direction.

Model and market diverge meaningfully here. The sportsbook consensus grants Portugal 52% to win—17 percentage points above the model's 35%—while compressing Colombia to 23%. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with the books. The dashboard offers no judgment on which source is more accurate; bettors should note the substantial disagreement between the statistical forecast and the betting market consensus.
